probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increases from 10% to 14%: compound stress now ELEVATED (two legs breached), Fear & Greed at 12, $805B equity wipeout, Brent approaching critical. The macro environment is actively hostile to risk assets. Base case decreases from 40% to 42% (slight reallocation within uncertainty range). Mid case increases from 38% to 33% — correction: mid should decrease given macro headwinds but RLUSD recovery and Ripple $3T clearing reference partially offset. Revised: Bear 14% (up from 10%), Base 42% (up from 40%), Mid 33% (down from 38%), Bull 11% (down from 12%). Net shift toward bear/base reflects ELEVATED compound stress and extreme fear, partially offset by RLUSD recovery and institutional signals.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Counter-thesis pressure has increased materially. Macro environment is actively hostile (Extreme Fear 12, equity wipeout, compound stress ELEVATED). Bitcoin losing $110B despite strong adoption news suggests institutional crypto sentiment is disconnecting from fundamentals. ODL data remains completely opaque — the '$3T clearing' reference is unverified and could mean cumulative historical volume rather than annualized throughput. Permissioned DEX has zero adoption 15 days after going live.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues incremental upgrades. No new data on institutional switching behavior. The 11,500-bank network effect remains the primary competitive moat — inertia alone is a formidable barrier to displacement.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new signals. Visa's B2B cross-border product remains active but hasn't scaled to threaten XRPL's target corridors.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: JPMorgan's institutional blockchain settlement continues expanding within its client base. The closed-network model competes directly for the same institutional clients Ripple targets.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new data on whether Nexus can operate independently without XRPL. The sovereign preference for central-bank-controlled settlement remains the strongest structural competitor to the XRP thesis.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ondo Finance pushing tokenized stocks narrative ('$100T+ equity markets, <1% onchain'). Ethereum institutional layer continues building parallel to XRPL for RWA tokenization. Competition for institutional mindshare is real.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data remains completely unavailable 15+ months after Ripple stopped publishing. The '$3T clearing' CoinDesk reference is unverified and ambiguous — could represent cumulative historical rather than annualized volume. This opacity is the single largest data gap in the thesis.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No token velocity data available. XRP 24h volume at $2.27B against $83.5B market cap yields a daily velocity ratio of 2.7% — within normal range but meaningless without institutional hold-time data. If XRP is used primarily as a bridge with sub-second hold times, utility value accrual to token holders remains questionable.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: $805B single-day equity wipeout, Fear & Greed at 12, Brent approaching $95 threatening stagflationary dynamics. The macro environment is moving toward recession-risk territory that would delay institutional crypto deployment timelines.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: Bitcoin losing $110B in a week despite strong adoption news is a classic late-cycle divergence signal. Price decoupling from fundamentals can precede extended downturns. Not confirmed but warning flag raised.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Crypto regulatory environment remains favorable — CBDC ban debate, SEC/CFTC consolidation signals, no anti-crypto legislative momentum. This is the strongest pillar of the thesis currently.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.13% with Brent approaching $95 creates inflationary pressure that could delay rate cuts or prompt renewed tightening talk. BOJ rate decision adds complexity. Fed policy path is uncertainty-maximizing.

bear_case.bear_narrative: Compound stress has escalated from MONITORING to ELEVATED — Brent crude's 17.5% surge to $92.87 puts the system $2.13 from CRITICAL, while Bessent's Russia sanctions float introduces a potential counter-catalyst that could resolve the tension within weeks. INCREASE_MONITORING. The thesis's central paradox is acute: institutional actors continue committing expensive, irreversible resources (7 ETFs, four-regulator registrations, equity stakes) while the load-bearing metric — ODL volume — remains a complete public data void that makes the core settlement claim unfalsifiable. Bear pressure at 60 reflects this honest uncertainty: compound stress escalation and RLUSD pace insufficiency against institutional infrastructure commitments and XRP's demonstrated resilience during the $805B equity wipe. The single data point that would most change this assessment is verified ODL volume — without it, the system is judging a building by its scaffolding while the foundation remains hidden.
